title: Ngo Quyen Prepares for the Battle of Bach Dang
summary: Ngo Quyen's brilliant strategy to defeat the enemy: preparing iron-tipped wooden stakes as 'underwater traps' in the river.

slides.text: Hey kids! When Ngo Quyen heard that the Southern Han army was about to sail their warships over, he wasn't worried at all. He had an incredibly powerful plan!
slides.imagePrompt: Ngô Quyền sitting with his generals around a model of the Bach Dang river, planning the stake trap, light of intelligence in his eyes, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He chose the lower reaches of the Bach Dang River - where there is a strong daily tidal cycle - as the site for his trap.
slides.imagePrompt: The Bach Dang river at sunset, showing the dramatic rise and fall of water levels, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Ngo Quyen mobilized thousands of soldiers and villagers to the forest to cut down large trees, sharpen them, and tip them with iron, turning them into giant arrows.
slides.imagePrompt: Soldiers and villagers working hard in the forest, sharpening giant wooden stakes and attaching iron tips, smoke from forges, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: These stakes were firmly hammered into the riverbed. When the tide rose, the water completely covered the tops of the stakes, creating a deadly invisible trap.
slides.imagePrompt: Stakes being hammered into the river bed, water rising to cover them, peaceful on the surface but dangerous below, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which location did Ngo Quyen choose for his battleground?
Why did Ngo Quyen have people sharpen and tip the wooden stakes with iron?
options: To prevent the stakes from rotting

slides.question: What natural element did Ngo Quyen use to hide the stake trap?
